content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me also read the style.css file to understand the existing styles. Let me also look at the README.md to understand the project context.

The issue is about adding language support for Cantonese (zh-HK) and English. The current implementation uses `navigator.language || 'en-US'` for both the main recognition and wake recognition. 

The approach should be:
1. Add a language selector in the Settings modal that lets users choose between:
   - English (en-US) 
   - Cantonese (zh-HK)
   - Auto-detect (use navigator.language)


2. Apply the selected language to both the main recognition and wake recognition instances.
3. Persist the language preference in localStorage.

The Web Speech API supports `en-US` for English and `zh-HK` for Cantonese, so I'll implement both auto-detection and user selection, with the auto-detect option being aware of Cantonese.

Now I'm checking the stylesheet to understand the styling patterns.

content.text: Now let me read the style.css to understand styling patterns.

content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the implementation:

1. **Language preference in Settings modal**: Add a language selector (a `<select>` dropdown) in the Settings modal that lets users choose between:
   - Auto (use browser default)
   - English (en-US)
   - Cantonese (zh-HK)

2. **Apply language to recognition**: When initializing recognition or wake recognition, read the stored language preference and apply it.

3. **Persist preference**: Save the language preference in localStorage.

4. **Re-init recognition when language changes**: When saving settings, re-initialize recognition with the new language.
   
Now I'll work through the implementation. I need to add the language selector to the settings modal in the HTML, then update the JavaScript to handle reading and applying the language preference, storing it in localStorage, and reinitializing the recognition engines whenever the language changes. Still writing select styles...

Now I need to wire up the JavaScript to handle language selection, storing the user's choice in localStorage and updating the recognition language when they change it.

input.command:
git add app.js index.html style.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(FRG-213): language support — Cantonese (zh-HK) and English

Add a Language selector to the Settings modal allowing users to choose
between Auto-detect (browser default), English (en-US), or Cantonese
(zh-HK / 廣東話). The selected language is persisted in localStorage and
applied to both the main SpeechRecognition instance and the wake-word
recogniser. Re-initialises recognition immediately when the setting
changes so users don't need to reload the page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create \
  --title "feat(FRG-213): language support — Cantonese (zh-HK) and English" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Adds a **Language** selector to the Settings modal with three options: Auto-detect, English (`en-US`), and Cantonese / 廣東話 (`zh-HK`)
- Preference is persisted in `localStorage` and applied to both the main `SpeechRecognition` instance and the wake-word recogniser via a new `getRecognitionLang()` helper
- When the setting changes while not actively recording, recognition is re-initialised immediately (no page reload required); wake-word listening is also restarted with the new language

## Test plan
- [ ] Open Settings → Language selector shows three options; defaults to Auto-detect on first install
- [ ] Select **Cantonese** → save → speak Cantonese; transcript is correctly recognised in zh-HK
- [ ] Select **English** → save → speak English; transcript is correctly recognised in en-US
- [ ] Select **Auto-detect** → save → recognition falls back to `navigator.language`
- [ ] Language preference survives a page reload (persisted in localStorage)
- [ ] With wake word enabled: changing language restarts wake recognition with the new locale
- [ ] Mixed-language input (code-switching between Cantonese and English) — recognised as well as the API allows

Closes FRG-213

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- **`index.html`** — Added a Language `<select>` (Auto-detect / English / Cantonese 廣東話) to the Settings modal body
- **`style.css`** — Extended `.modal-body input` styles to also apply to `select`; added custom arrow, option background, and focus ring for the dropdown
- **`app.js`** — Added `langSelect` DOM ref; added `getRecognitionLang()` helper that reads `localStorage('recognitionLang')` with `navigator.language` fallback; applied it to `initRecognition()` and `initWakeRecognition()`; wired `openSettings()` to restore the saved value; extended `saveSettings` handler to persist the selection and re-initialise recognition (plus wake-word restart) when the language changes

## Key Decisions
- **User-selectable over auto-detect-only**: The issue allows either approach; a selector gives users explicit control, which is more reliable than relying on `navigator.language` for bilingual users
- **Re-init on save (not on recording start)**: Language is applied at `new SpeechRecognition()` time, so re-initialising immediately after save ensures the next recording uses the new locale without a page reload; if recording is active we skip re-init (the next natural start will pick it up)
- **Wake recognition re-started with new lang**: Both recogniser instances share `getRecognitionLang()` so Cantonese wake-phrase detection works correctly when the locale is set

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/FRG-213-language-support-cantonese-english`
- PR: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill/pull/7

## Key Decisions
- **User-selectable over auto-detect-only**: The issue allows either approach; a selector gives users explicit control, which is more reliable than relying on `navigator.language` for bilingual users
- **Re-init on save (not on recording start)**: Language is applied at `new SpeechRecognition()` time, so re-initialising immediately after save ensures the next recording uses the new locale without a page reload; if recording is active we skip re-init (the next natural start will pick it up)
- **Wake recognition re-started with new lang**: Both recogniser instances share `getRecognitionLang()` so Cantonese wake-phrase detection works correctly when the locale is set

## PR / Branch
